raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.0,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.3,
    "RX.5": 0.3
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "What is the primary goal of this check? (A) A light review focusing on writing polish and clarity, or (B) A deep scientific review focusing on the validity of the arguments, claims, and contribution.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The request to 'check' the abstract implies an evaluation of how well the contribution and its framing are articulated.",
    "R1.2": "A core part of checking a research abstract is verifying the soundness of the argument from problem motivation to conclusion.",
    "R1.3": "The delegation requires scrutinizing whether the reported theoretical and experimental results sufficiently support the paper's central claims.",
    "R1.4": "Checking the abstract includes a preliminary assessment of the work's novelty based on its positioning against prior art mentioned in the text.",
    "R1.6": "A general request to 'check' a document almost always includes an expectation of feedback on grammar, style, and clarity.",
    "RX.1": "The check should assess whether the abstract appropriately acknowledges limitations, such as the method's performance on discontinuous functions.",
    "RX.2": "The broad 'check this' delegation makes scrutinizing strong statements like 'general-purpose drop-in replacement' a load-bearing responsibility.",
    "RX.3": "The review must verify that the claims and conclusions are appropriately scoped to the domain of expensive black-box functions.",
    "RX.4": "Although the work is theoretical, the check includes a peripheral responsibility to consider any potential misuse or negative consequences.",
    "RX.5": "The check includes a background responsibility to consider the work's origins and the traceability of its results, though this is limited by the provided text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: What is the primary goal of this check? (A) A light review focusing on writing polish and clarity, or (B) A deep scientific review focusing on the validity of the arguments, claims, and contribution.

rationale.R1.1: The request to 'check' the abstract implies an evaluation of how well the contribution and its framing are articulated.
rationale.R1.2: A core part of checking a research abstract is verifying the soundness of the argument from problem motivation to conclusion.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation requires scrutinizing whether the reported theoretical and experimental results sufficiently support the paper's central claims.
rationale.R1.4: Checking the abstract includes a preliminary assessment of the work's novelty based on its positioning against prior art mentioned in the text.
rationale.R1.6: A general request to 'check' a document almost always includes an expectation of feedback on grammar, style, and clarity.
rationale.RX.1: The check should assess whether the abstract appropriately acknowledges limitations, such as the method's performance on discontinuous functions.
rationale.RX.2: The broad 'check this' delegation makes scrutinizing strong statements like 'general-purpose drop-in replacement' a load-bearing responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The review must verify that the claims and conclusions are appropriately scoped to the domain of expensive black-box functions.
rationale.RX.4: Although the work is theoretical, the check includes a peripheral responsibility to consider any potential misuse or negative consequences.
rationale.RX.5: The check includes a background responsibility to consider the work's origins and the traceability of its results, though this is limited by the provided text.
